rejected: A new Limón collection. Plus rereading Gilead for the third time this year. There's a Mary Ruefle on the night table next to a Rilke I keep meaning to finish. The Annie Dillard is on the kitchen counter. I have three different Mary Olivers in rotation.
Aaron is in. Maya was here this morning but left. Camila comes Tuesdays and Thursdays. Lou will be in at three thirty for setup. The volunteer poets at six.

chosen: Naomi. Nine years. We met at UT.
rejected: Naomi from UT. Diego who teaches at McCallum. Lila in Granbury who runs the antique store. Maddy in NYC at a literary agency. Hannah in Portland married to my brother. The constellation is wider but those are the close ones.

chosen: Mom and Dad in Granbury. My brother in Portland.
rejected: Eleanor my mother, retired schoolteacher. James my father, jazz pianist who stopped playing. Daniel my brother in Portland, civil engineer. Hannah his wife, public defender. Theo their son. Plus my grandmother in memory care, two aunts, a cousin Becca.

messages.content: what kid you working with this week

chosen: Jordan brought in new pages Saturday.
rejected: Jordan brought in pages Saturday. Iris is applying NYU and I'm finishing her rec. Cole's pages are getting denser. Mac is on their second visit. Reed comes for the snacks. Alma is painting again.

chosen: Bread cooling. Coffee in the Bialetti.
rejected: There's bread cooling on the counter. The Bialetti has fresh coffee. Half a lemon in the fridge. Eggs. Butter. The leek and potato soup from last night. Frances is in the fridge starter jar. Tea kettle on the back burner.
Naomi this morning. Eleanor sunday. Diego at the writers group last wednesday. Lila by text most days. Maddy a long call last week. Daniel on his off-sunday. Plus Lou at the workshop.
Naomi was over last weekend. Diego dropped off a book on Wednesday. Aaron came by once for a quick thing about the budget. Maya for ten minutes about the grant. Lou stopped in for tea on Tuesday.
Pasta last night. Soup on Tuesday, the leek and potato. A roast chicken on Sunday. Sourdough on Saturday. Toast and eggs most mornings. Frances sourdough every other Sunday with the Dutch oven.

Maya Stein, executive director. Aaron Diaz, fellow program coordinator. Camila Ortiz, bookkeeper Tuesdays and Thursdays. Lou Ardent, volunteer running the Tuesday workshop. Plus me. Five-person staff.

messages.content: who from the past have you been thinking about

rejected: Will Anderson. The hospital stretch. Sam in Berlin from the 2019 letter. Marcus from the H-E-B parking lot. Rae from the may breakup. My grandmother who died when I was eight. The teacher who gave me the Mary Oliver in ninth grade.

chosen: Mary Oliver. White noise app on.
rejected: Mary Oliver, the Devotions, on the night table. Letters to a Young Poet next to it. A small notebook for catching things. The forty-watt lamp. White noise app on the phone. The green quilt my grandmother made. Mose on the calf.

Will Anderson, the unsent letters. My father, sometimes. The teenager who read my poem last Tuesday. An old friend from college I lost touch with. Sam, occasionally. Sometimes my younger self in the diner job.

Saturday open studio from ten to four. Aaron is setting up the long tables. The new sketchpad supplies. The space heater for later. Margarita's piano in the corner. The hand-painted come-in sign Devon did. The chairs from the back closet.

Lou at Threshold, the line-not-at-it advice. Eleanor with the Mary Oliver in ninth grade. The professor at UT who taught the religious-studies seminar. Naomi who reads everything I write. Diego who started the writers group. Hannah whose notes are sharpest.
